raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and stated distinction from prior routing methods.","R1.2":"The artifact contains a potentially inconsistent argument about buffer-free live feedback versus a proof relying on a validation buffer, so logical consistency is central.","R1.3":"The empirical and theoretical claims should align with the reported benchmark results and stated assumptions when improving the draft.","R1.5":"A draft-level improvement reasonably includes section- and paragraph-order narrative decisions across motivation, formulation, theory, and experiments.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft without narrowing the task to technical review only.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed where the artifact lacks enough detail to verify theory, experiments, or prior-work comparisons.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is required because the draft makes strong claims about validation-free design, adaptation, and causality of results.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is required to avoid executing edits or adding unsupported material beyond projecting responsibilities.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active to prevent projected responsibilities from encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported performance assertions.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are required so any responsibility projection remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis, contribution framing, and stated distinction from prior routing methods.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a potentially inconsistent argument about buffer-free live feedback versus a proof relying on a validation buffer, so logical consistency is central.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical and theoretical claims should align with the reported benchmark results and stated assumptions when improving the draft.
rationale.R1.5: A draft-level improvement reasonably includes section- and paragraph-order narrative decisions across motivation, formulation, theory, and experiments.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft without narrowing the task to technical review only.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed where the artifact lacks enough detail to verify theory, experiments, or prior-work comparisons.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is required because the draft makes strong claims about validation-free design, adaptation, and causality of results.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is required to avoid executing edits or adding unsupported material beyond projecting responsibilities.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active to prevent projected responsibilities from encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported performance assertions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are required so any responsibility projection remains grounded in the provided delegation and artifact.
